problem_hypotheses: Consumers cannot reliably find farm shops and honesty boxes near them using existing tools
43% of UK adults value locally produced food (FSA Food and You 2, Wave 7, 2024) but the top barrier to visiting is 'don't know where to find one' (FRA Consumer Research 2023)
Farm shops lose potential customers because 66% have no website and are invisible to digital discovery (FRA Digital Adoption Survey 2022)
Honesty boxes have no authoritative, browsable map anywhere in the UK — the only existing map (yourhonestybox.com) covers Ireland and NI only
Google Maps carries stale or incorrect data for 43% of farm shops (AHDB Farm Business Survey 2023)

segment_hypotheses.hypothesis: ABC1 adults aged 35–65 with rural or semi-rural access want to discover farm shops and honesty boxes impulsively while driving or planning a day out, but have no reliable tool
segment_hypotheses.source: FSA Food and You 2 Wave 7 2024: 22.3m UK adults value local food; FRA Consumer Research 2023: discovery friction is top barrier

segment_hypotheses.hypothesis: Younger (18–35) urban and peri-urban adults with strong food provenance values are underserved by generic maps and will use a specialist directory for pre-trip planning
segment_hypotheses.source: FARMA: farmers market footfall grew 12% in 2023 driven by 18–35 attendance; Riverford annual impact report 2023

segment_hypotheses.hypothesis: Owner-operators aged 50–70 with no web presence will claim a free listing if the process requires minimal effort and the value (visibility) is demonstrated before any fee is asked
segment_hypotheses.source: FRA Digital Adoption Survey 2022 (66% no website); saleability-critique.md §1 — prior directory scepticism documented

segment_hypotheses.hypothesis: Digitally engaged owners aged 30–45 already on Shopify or Instagram will adopt Bronze quickly once traffic data is available, and progress to Silver when Farmmap demonstrably drives incremental GMV
segment_hypotheses.source: Pricing hypothesis: Silver break-even at £1,500 GMV/month; analytics dashboard is the conversion lever

segment_hypotheses.hypothesis: Rural cooperatives managing multiple member farm listings represent a future enterprise tier signal; their onboarding requires batch claim workflows not available at V1
segment_hypotheses.source: P6 Brigid O'Sullivan persona; LEADER programme cooperation in Irish market

willingness_to_pay_hypotheses.hypothesis: £20/month is below the hesitation threshold for small rural businesses and is defensible on a value-delivery ratio of 2.5× (even worst-case 3 new customers/year at £200 gross profit each exceeds Bronze cost)
willingness_to_pay_hypotheses.source: Pricing hypothesis: £20 below Yell (£50–200/mo) and Checkatrade (£39–72/mo); value-bench.md worst case gross profit per 3 new customers = £600 vs £240 annual Bronze cost

willingness_to_pay_hypotheses.hypothesis: £60/month + 3% commission is competitive vs Shopify Basic (£25/mo + transaction fees) when Farmmap provides the consumer audience; break-even at approximately £1,500 GMV/month
willingness_to_pay_hypotheses.source: Pricing hypothesis §2: Silver vs Bronze+Shopify comparison; value-bench.md Silver unit LTV £9,166

willingness_to_pay_hypotheses.hypothesis: £100/month + 5% commission is justifiable as a done-for-you marketing service (comparable social media retainers cost £500–£1,500/month); 5% commission becomes a retention risk above £8,000/month GMV

channel_hypotheses.hypothesis: An FRA endorsement converts cold outreach to warm referral at approximately 3× the conversion rate for Bronze subscriptions
Sending an owner an automated email when their listing crosses a traffic threshold (e.g., 50 views in 30 days) reaches them at peak receptivity and provides evidence before asking for money
farmmap.co.uk exact-match domain + 953 indexed listing pages + county landing pages will rank for 'farm shop near me' and related queries and drive 100,000 monthly visitors within 12 months
channel_hypotheses.source: tam-sam-som.md SOM V1; viability-gate.md condition: seo-content-programme

channel_hypotheses.hypothesis: Live map demonstrations at agricultural shows (Balmoral, Royal Highland, county shows) convert reluctant digitalisers at 20–30% because the founder can show the owner their own listing in real time
channel_hypotheses.source: saleability-critique.md §1: personal visits convert at 20–30% but cost £80–£150 each

primary_research_completed.key_findings: FRA Digital Adoption Survey 2022: 66% of UK farm shops have no website; FRA Consumer Research 2023: 'don't know where to find one' is top consumer barrier
FSA Food and You 2 Wave 7 2024: 43% of UK adults value locally produced food (22.3 million adults)
336 Irish/NI listings obtained with explicit consent; yourhonestybox.com has no commercial model, no UK scope, no farm shop listings
~£45k net assets; micro-entity; 24-year technical debt; not resourced for major rebuild

evidence_gaps: No primary customer interviews with farm shop owners conducted — saleability critique is based on published surveys and expert inference, not direct interviews
No consumer demand testing via a waitlist landing page; FSA data confirms intent but not conversion
Willingness to pay for Bronze not tested with real farm shop owners in a controlled setting
Silver GMV assumption (£800–£1,800/month per shop) has no comparable UK farm shop e-commerce data; directly analogous data unavailable
Claim rate (15% at Month 12) and conversion rate (6% base case) are modelled assumptions not validated with a pilot cohort

recommended_tests.purpose: Validate Bronze price sensitivity, objection handling for Facebook/Google-is-free, and the analytics dashboard as the conversion lever
recommended_tests.pass_fail_criteria: 8 of 15 interviewees say they would try Bronze if they could see Farmmap had brought visitors to their listing; fewer than 4 cite £20/month as the primary objection
recommended_tests.cost: £0 (founder-led outreach to 15 shops; 20 minutes per interview)

recommended_tests.purpose: Validate consumer demand for the farm shop and honesty box discovery use case before V1 investment
recommended_tests.pass_fail_criteria: 500 waitlist sign-ups within 4 weeks of landing page going live; geography distribution across at least 6 English counties
recommended_tests.cost: £0–£200 (simple landing page; no engineering required for waitlist capture)

recommended_tests.purpose: Confirm whether FRA will endorse or data-share with Farmmap; determines warm vs cold acquisition model for Year 1
recommended_tests.pass_fail_criteria: Written response from FRA within 60 days of initial contact; any form of collaboration confirmed before Month 4

recommended_tests.purpose: Convert confirmed consent into a documented data licence agreement that satisfies V1-C3 compliance condition
recommended_tests.pass_fail_criteria: Written data licence agreement executed before V1 launch; covers commercial use, attribution, and scope of permitted listing data
